title: Get Polymarket Builder Program approval
description: Waiting on Polymarket team to verify Builder account. Blocking all trading functionality.

title: Bug: Portfolio position links don't navigate to market detail page
description:
Clicking a position in Portfolio page does nothing - should navigate to /markets/[market_id].

Example: 'Will Bitcoin reach 95,000 by December 31, 2025?' position is visible but not clickable.

Previous context: There was an issue with how market purchases were recording to database, causing problems with portfolio. Database may have been added but unclear if fix applies to existing positions.

Investigate:
- frontend/src/app/portfolio/page.tsx
- frontend/src/components/portfolio/
- How market_id is stored in positions table
- onClick handlers for position cards
- Whether market_id is valid/present in position records
close_reason: Fixed: Portfolio position links used <Link href='/app/markets/...'> which double-prefixes with Next.js basePath config. Changed to href='/markets/...' (basePath adds /app automatically). Also fixed Data API field mapping in useSafeAddress hook (checks slug/market/marketSlug and conditionId/condition_id fields) and fixed Quick Actions links.

title: Bug: Multi-outcome markets display placeholder names and broken data
description:
Markets with >4 outcomes show incorrect data:
- Candidate names display as 'Person P', 'Person S', etc. instead of real names
- Price history chart shows 'No price history available'
- All outcomes show 50.0¢/50.0¢ instead of real prices
- Markets with exactly 4 outcomes work correctly (e.g., Fed decision)

Root cause likely in top-4 outcome filtering logic that was added to limit chart lines.

Examples:
- Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028 (128 outcomes) - BROKEN
- Presidential Election Winner 2028 (128 outcomes) - BROKEN  
- Fed decision in January? (4 outcomes) - WORKS

Investigate:
- frontend/src/app/markets/[id]/page.tsx
- backend/integrations/polymarket.py
- Any outcome filtering/limiting logic
- How outcome names are fetched from Polymarket API
close_reason: Fixed: Backend now detects incomplete nested market data from Gamma events API and fetches individual markets when needed. Also returns multi-outcome data directly without calling _parse_market_prices() which was overwriting prices with 0.5 defaults. Trending endpoint now includes clobTokenIds in outcomes for price chart.
